title: braya glabella
intro: Braya glabella, also known as the pale mountain avens, is a rare and perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which have been shown to aid in reducing pain and swelling, as well as protecting against cell damage. The health benefits of Braya glabella include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant properties, and its ability to act as a natural antimicrobial agent to combat infections. The herbal preparations of Braya glabella typically involve drying and processing the plant's leaves and flowers to create teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
